title: Crispy Malaysian Chicken Casserole
description: A savory, aerated casserole with a hint of sweet black treacle, cooked in a fondue pot for a nostalgic Malaysian twist.

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: with 1 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon curry powder, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper

instructions: Heat the fondue pot over medium heat and add the coconut oil, letting it melt fully.
Add the diced chicken to the pot and sauté until lightly browned on all sides.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, diced onion, sliced carrots, and celery, cooking until vegetables soften.
Pour in the coconut milk and chicken broth, stirring to combine.
Add the black treacle, turmeric, curry powder, salt, and pepper; bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the casserole cook uncovered for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is fully cooked and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, then remove from heat.
Transfer the casserole into a serving dish, garnish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime if desired.
Serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread.
